title: Reading and Use of English - Part 8
description: Read the four texts about art collecting. For questions 47 – 56, choose from the collectors (A – D).

texts.A:
### The Investor (Marcus)
Marcus views art primarily as an asset class. [51]'I don't need to like a painting to buy it; I just need to know it will appreciate in value,' he says.[/51] [48]He admits that he keeps most of his collection in a high-security, climate-controlled warehouse rather than in his home.[/48] [55]He points out that the art market is one of the few remaining 'unregulated' financial frontiers, which makes it both exciting and dangerous.[/55] [50]He warns that for every masterpiece that skyrockets in price, there are a thousand works that become worthless overnight.[/50]
texts.B:
### The Enthusiast (Lydia)
Lydia only buys art that she has a 'visceral' connection with. [47]She mentions that she once saved money for a year just to buy a small sketch by an unknown artist because it reminded her of her childhood.[/47] [53]She is critical of people who buy art just for prestige, arguing that it's a form of 'cultural posturing'.[/53] [56]For her, the joy is in the daily interaction with the work; she rotates the paintings in her living room to see them in different lights.[/56] [52]She believes that you don't own a piece of art; you are just its temporary guardian.[/52]
texts.C:
### The Patron (David)
David focuses on supporting emerging artists from developing countries. [49]He mentions that his goal is to provide a platform for voices that are often ignored by the mainstream Western art world.[/49] [54]He highlights the difficulty of transporting fragile works across borders, often dealing with complex customs regulations.[/54] [51]He doesn't care about resale value; in fact, he often donates pieces to public museums after a few years.[/51] [50]He mentions that the most rewarding part is seeing an artist he supported early on get their first major solo exhibition.[/50]
texts.D:
### The Digital Collector (Ava)
Ava collects NFTs (Non-Fungible Tokens) and digital media. [52]She argues that the traditional art world is elitist and that digital platforms allow anyone to become a collector.[/52] [48]She admits that her parents find her hobby 'insane' because she is paying for something that doesn't physically exist.[/48] [53]However, she highlights the 'transparency' of the blockchain, where you can see the entire ownership history of a work with one click.[/53] [55]She acknowledges the environmental criticism of crypto-art but believes that the move to more eco-friendly systems is inevitable.[/55] [47]She enjoys the social community of other digital collectors online.[/47]

questions.question: mention a long-term personal sacrifice made to acquire a specific work?
questions.explanation: Lydia saved money for a year to buy a small sketch.

questions.question: admit that their collection is not easily accessible for viewing?
questions.explanation: Marcus keeps his collection in a warehouse. (Ava D mentions it's digital/not physical, but Marcus is the one mentioning a secure location).

questions.question: state that their primary motivation is to give exposure to underrepresented talent?
questions.explanation: David wants to provide a platform for ignored voices.

questions.question: refer to a sense of pride in the professional growth of someone they have helped?
questions.explanation: David finds it rewarding to see an artist he supported get a major exhibition.

questions.question: claim that the aesthetic appeal of a work is irrelevant to their purchase decision?
questions.explanation: Marcus says he doesn't need to like a painting to buy it.

questions.question: view themselves as having a responsibility towards the work rather than absolute ownership?
questions.explanation: Lydia believes you are just a 'temporary guardian'.

questions.question: mention a technical advantage of their chosen method of collecting?
questions.explanation: Ava mentions the transparency of the blockchain for ownership history.

questions.question: complain about the logistical hurdles of moving art internationally?
questions.explanation: David mentions the difficulty of customs and transporting fragile works.

questions.question: acknowledge a controversial or negative aspect of their area of collecting?
questions.explanation: Marcus mentions the unregulated/dangerous market; Ava (D) acknowledges the environmental criticism.

questions.question: describe a habit of changing the arrangement of their art to gain new perspectives?
questions.explanation: Lydia rotates the paintings in her living room.
